summary: Draft PR against plbonus that was closed without merge. It replaced the `/// #fn_80039618` placeholder with a large C implementation for player bonus/stat flag calculation, typed more of `pl_804D6470_t`, added a `dont_inline` guard around `fn_80038700`, and adjusted `fn_8003CC84` digit arithmetic. The decomp-dev bot reported +1640 matched code bytes, +456 matched data bytes, full matches for plbonus `.data`, `.sdata2`, and `fn_8003CC84`, plus `fn_80039618` reaching 99.67%, but there was no human review feedback in the slice.

key_files.role: Implemented `fn_80039618`, protected `fn_80038700` from inlining, and changed `fn_8003CC84` arithmetic for matching.
Refined `pl_804D6470_t` from byte padding at several offsets into typed float/u32/int threshold fields used by plbonus.

what_changed: Added a full C body for `fn_80039618(int player)`, using `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2`, `Player_GetActionStats`, `Player_GetEntity`, stale move table counters, action stats, falls, KOs, and game-mode helpers to set many bonus flags via `setFlag`.
Typed previously opaque parts of `pl_804D6470_t`: examples include `x0`, `x8`, `x10`, `x20`, and `x160` as floats, and `xC`, `x50`, `x54`, and `xF0` as integer thresholds.
Wrapped `fn_80038700` in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`, preserving it as a separate helper for summing ranges.
Adjusted `fn_8003CC84` decimal digit extraction from `% 100` expressions to quotient/subtraction expressions: `(temp_ret - (temp_r5 * 100)) / 10` and the corresponding ones digit calculation.
Bot report says plbonus `.data` became 100%, `.sdata2` became 100%, `fn_8003CC84` became 100%, and `fn_80039618` improved from 0.00% to 99.67%.
The PR was draft, had no body, no reviews, no review comments, and was closed unmerged, so the slice does not show final acceptance or rejection rationale.
smart_moves: The implementation caches many `plActionStats` fields and table pointers into locals before the branch-heavy logic, which is a common register-allocation/control-flow tactic for matching large functions.

The code keeps small scoped blocks for independent computations such as special-move sums, combo count min/max, hit-count classification, ratio accumulation, and KO streak checks, helping constrain local lifetimes.
It uses explicit labels/gotos such as `skip_only_low_moves` and `skip_combo_count` to model early loop exits rather than forcing structured C that might produce different branches.
The PR refines only the `pl_804D6470_t` offsets needed by observed accesses while retaining offset-style names, avoiding overclaiming semantics for threshold data.
The `%`-to-subtraction rewrite in `fn_8003CC84` is a precise codegen-focused change that produced a full function match according to the bot report.
`PAD_STACK(16)` was added at the end of `fn_80039618`, indicating attention to stack-frame matching beyond source-level behavior.
decomp_lessons: For large stat/bonus routines, first expose threshold/config struct fields at the exact offsets the function reads; even offset-style typed fields can unlock code and data matches.
Do not assume idiomatic C is best for matching: explicit subtraction after division can beat `%` when reconstructing compiler output.
Tiny helper functions may need `#pragma dont_inline` once a large caller is decompiled, especially if inlining would change both the helper and caller matches.
Branch shape matters in long classifier functions; negated conditions with `break`, labels, and carefully ordered `else if` threshold ladders may be necessary to reproduce original codegen.
When evidence for semantics is weak, keep address-based function names and `xNN` field names while using descriptive locals to make the decomp readable.
Because this PR closed unmerged and `fn_80039618` was only 99.67%, reuse these tactics as leads and compare against later merged plbonus work before treating the implementation as final.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based unknown names such as `fn_80039618`, `fn_80038700`, `pl_80038628`, and `pl_80037B2C`.
Used offset-style struct member names in `pl_804D6470_t` such as `x0`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x20`, `x50`, `x54`, `xF0`, and `x160`, while improving their C types.

Used descriptive local names for inferred counters and aggregates: `aerials`, `atk_thrown`, `hit_thrown`, `attacks_total`, `hits_total`, `x358_total`, `found_low`, `found_high`, `sum_attacks`, `sum_x358`, `max_ratio`, and `sum_ratio`.
Relied on existing accessor naming style with `Player_GetActionStats`, `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2`, `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_GetFalls`, and `Player_GetKOsByPlayerIndex`.
Kept partially understood stat group names like `x358_hits`, `x1A8`, `x1A0_count`, and stale move table offsets rather than inventing semantic labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma pop` around `fn_80038700` to prevent unwanted inlining/codegen drift.
Changed modulo digit extraction to quotient/subtract arithmetic in `fn_8003CC84`.
Used explicit casts in comparisons, including `(f32)(max - min)`, `(f32) max * pl_804D6470->x28`, `(s32) table->x0_staleMoveTable.xC64`, and `(u32)` casts around table fields.
Used pointer walking over `x358_counts` with `u32* p = &x358_counts[1]`, `*p`, and `p++` for the 99-entry loop.
Preserved non-idiomatic branch forms such as `if (!(...)) { break; }` and labeled skip targets to match original control flow.
Repeated some accessor expressions even when a local `table` exists, likely to influence register lifetime/codegen, though the slice does not explicitly document intent.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments are present in the slice.
The only comment is the decomp-dev bot report for GALE01: matched code 67.81% (+0.04%, +1640 bytes) and matched data 39.68% (+0.04%, +456 bytes).
Bot-reported new/full matches: `main/melee/pl/plbonus` `.data` +400 bytes to 100%, `fn_8003CC84` +34 bytes to 100%, and `.sdata2` +24 bytes to 100%.
Bot-reported partial improvement: `fn_80039618` +6678 bytes from 0.00% to 99.67%.
PR metadata shows it was a draft and closed without merge; the slice does not explain why.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `fn_80039618` to see whether the remaining 0.33% was completed and merged.
Search master/current history for `pl_804D6470_t` to verify which offset field types and names survived review.
Compare the final `fn_8003CC84` implementation to see whether the subtraction-based digit extraction remained necessary.
Look for follow-up review or PR discussion explaining why draft PR #2523 was closed unmerged.
Investigate semantic names for the `setFlag` bonus IDs used throughout `fn_80039618` before renaming fields or flags.
